messages: did you know? you cannot burp in space the same way you do on Earth because gas and liquid do not separate normally in microgravity.
tip: in Minecraft, always keep an escape route before you take a fight.
did you know? honey never really spoils; archaeologists have found edible honey in ancient tombs.
tip: never trust a player just because they say they are friendly.
did you know? octopuses have three hearts.
tip: if someone is building too close to you, expect a trap or a push.
did you know? bananas are berries, but strawberries are not.
tip: keep healing items on your hotbar so you can react fast.
did you know? a day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus.
tip: fight near terrain you control, not in open ground.
did you know? sharks have been around longer than trees.
tip: break line of sight when you need to heal or reset a fight.
did you know? Wombats make cube-shaped poop.
tip: do not stand still while eating or healing.
did you know? lightning can heat the air to about 30,000°C, which is hotter than the Sun’s surface.
tip: keep blocks ready so you can block off a chase path quickly.
did you know? the Eiffel Tower can grow taller in hot weather because metal expands.
tip: if a tunnel looks too perfect, check for a trap before entering.
did you know? some turtles can breathe through their butts underwater.
tip: use corners and doorways to control close fights.
did you know? ants can survive in surprisingly extreme environments.
tip: do not chase too far if you might get third-partied.
did you know? there are more possible games of chess than atoms in the observable universe.
tip: keep your inventory organized so you can swap items without thinking.
did you know? the human brain uses roughly 20% of the body’s energy.
tip: always watch behind you when looting after a kill.
did you know? freshwater jellyfish exist.
tip: use high ground when you can, especially for bows or ranged pressure.
did you know? some bamboo species can grow more than a meter in a single day.
tip: if someone retreats too smoothly, they may be baiting you.
did you know? the Moon moves about 3.8 centimeters away from Earth each year.
tip: keep one hotbar slot ready for emergency movement or blocks.
did you know? a group of flamingos is called a flamboyance.
tip: do not overcommit when your gear or health is weak.
did you know? penguins have waterproof feathers.
tip: if you are low, retreat first and heal second.
did you know? the longest English word in major dictionaries is often listed as a chemical name for a protein.
tip: bait greedy players into bad positioning before committing.
did you know? a bolt of lightning can be about 5 times hotter than the Sun’s surface.
tip: use terrain changes to interrupt enemy movement.
did you know? sea otters hold hands while sleeping so they do not drift apart.
tip: watch for weird block placements that hint at a hidden setup.
did you know? koalas have fingerprints that can look very similar to humans.
tip: keep one route for escape and one for re-entry.
did you know? some turtles live over 100 years.
tip: never assume a player is safe just because they stop moving.
did you know? the Great Wall of China is not a single continuous wall; it is a system of walls and fortifications.
tip: if you are in a fight, make the enemy fight where you want them to.
did you know? Venus rotates in the opposite direction to most planets in the Solar System.
tip: learn to strafe and change pace so you are harder to hit.
did you know? hummingbirds can remember every flower they visited.
tip: use fake confidence to bait overpushes when you are prepared.
did you know? a day on Mercury is longer than its year.
tip: do not tunnel vision one target and forget your surroundings.
did you know? some frogs can freeze and survive, then thaw out later.
tip: keep your best gear safe instead of risking it every fight.
did you know? the Sahara was once a much greener place.
tip: when fleeing, place blocks behind you to slow pursuit.
did you know? a teaspoon of neutron star matter would weigh billions of tons on Earth.
tip: check corners before crossing open areas.
did you know? jellyfish have existed for hundreds of millions of years.
tip: if a player keeps looking away and back, they may be setting a trap.
did you know? the smallest bone in the human body is in the ear.
tip: never enter a suspicious base without a backup plan.
did you know? some trees communicate with each other through fungal networks underground.
tip: keep your movement unpredictable during every fight.
did you know? the blue whale is the largest animal ever known to have existed.
tip: use knockback and spacing to your advantage in close combat.
did you know? there are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way.
tip: heal early instead of waiting until you are almost dead.
did you know? the Pacific Ocean is larger than all of Earth’s land area combined.
tip: trust should be earned through actions, not words.
did you know? some species of frogs can glow under ultraviolet light.
tip: if a fight feels bad, reset it instead of forcing it.
